title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Here’s what happened in crypto today
briefing: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: Stanford study says 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets enable settlement manipulation
summary: Stanford study says 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets enable settlement manipulation
briefing: Researchers found that Polymarket’s five-minute Bitcoin prediction markets create incentives to manipulate spot prices around contract settlement, proposing longer settlement windows as a potential fix.

title: Revolut receives in-principle approval from UAE authorities for crypto services
summary: Revolut receives in-principle approval from UAE authorities for crypto services
briefing: Dubai’s Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority approved the London-headquartered fintech company for broker-dealer, management and investment, and exchange services in the UAE.

title: US Senator blasts AG pick for ‘dismantling’ crypto unit, Trump’s CZ pardon
summary: US Senator blasts AG pick for ‘dismantling’ crypto unit, Trump’s CZ pardon
briefing: President Donald Trump’s pick for US Attorney General faced criticism from members of the Senate Judiciary Committee on his approach to crypto enforcement and the pardon of former Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao.

title: Ostium pauses trading as security firms report multimillion-dollar oracle exploit
summary: Ostium pauses trading as security firms report multimillion-dollar oracle exploit
briefing: Ostium halted trading and advised revoking contract approvals after blockchain security firms reported an apparent oracle-related exploit of its OLP liquidity vault, with estimated losses ranging from $18 million to $22 million.

title: Aave launches V4 on Avalanche, laying groundwork for tokenized credit markets
summary: Aave launches V4 on Avalanche, laying groundwork for tokenized credit markets
briefing: The deployment marks Aave V4’s first expansion beyond Ethereum and introduces lending infrastructure designed to support future markets for tokenized real-world assets.

title: Securitize, Cantor target tokenized IPOs for public markets
summary: Securitize, Cantor target tokenized IPOs for public markets
briefing: Securitize and Cantor Fitzgerald are developing infrastructure for tokenized IPOs and secondary equity offerings within the existing US securities framework.

title: Bitcoin hits $65.5K as more surprise US inflation data sparks three-week BTC price high
summary: Bitcoin hits $65.5K as more surprise US inflation data sparks three-week BTC price high
briefing: Bitcoin moved up to its highest levels since June 22 as US PPI inflation numbers provided the week’s second surprise macro data drop.

title: Why Analysts Aren’t Worried About Coinbase’s 30% Drop
summary: Why Analysts Aren’t Worried About Coinbase’s 30% Drop
briefing: William Blair slashed earnings estimates by 34% and held its Outperform call. But analysts think Bitcoin's chart may already be answering the bigger question.

title: OpenAI Uses AI Red Team to Strengthen GPT-5.6 Against Prompt Injection Attacks
summary: OpenAI Uses AI Red Team to Strengthen GPT-5.6 Against Prompt Injection Attacks
briefing: OpenAI said its new automated red-teaming model, GPT-Red, uncovered vulnerabilities that were used to make GPT-5.6 more resistant to prompt injection attacks.

title: Meet Bonsai: The First 27B AI Model That Fits on Your Phone
summary: Meet Bonsai: The First 27B AI Model That Fits on Your Phone
briefing: PrismML's Bonsai 27B runs full reasoning AI on an iPhone for free. We tested it, and it's as impressive as it sounds.

title: What Is BIP-110 and Why Is It Dividing the Bitcoin Community?
summary: What Is BIP-110 and Why Is It Dividing the Bitcoin Community?
briefing: A proposal to restrict non-financial data on Bitcoin has split prominent developers, miners, and industry leaders ahead of a key activation deadline, reopening one of the network's biggest governance debates since the Blocksize Wars.

title: Base Creator Jesse Pollak Steps Back From App Leadership After Admitting Social Bet 'Was Wrong'
summary: Base Creator Jesse Pollak Steps Back From App Leadership After Admitting Social Bet 'Was Wrong'
briefing: Pollak said he is handing the Base App back to Coinbase to focus on building Base into "the blockchain for global finance" after conceding the network's push into on-chain social missed the mark.

title: BlackRock, Goldman, and JP Morgan Will Give Tokenized Stocks a Try
summary: BlackRock, Goldman, and JP Morgan Will Give Tokenized Stocks a Try
briefing: The Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation is working with nearly 40 financial firms in a pilot to test tokenized stocks and U.S. treasuries.

title: Another DeFi Exploit: Perp DEX Ostium Loses $18 Million in Oracle Attack
summary: Another DeFi Exploit: Perp DEX Ostium Loses $18 Million in Oracle Attack
briefing: Hackers manipulated Ostium's price feed by compromising an oracle signer key, allowing them to drain roughly $18 million from the Arbitrum-based perpetuals exchange.

title: US Treasury Freezes $131 Million in Iran-Linked Crypto Wallets
summary: US Treasury Freezes $131 Million in Iran-Linked Crypto Wallets
briefing: OFAC sanctioned addresses tied to Iran's central bank and armed forces, with Tether locking four Tron wallets as Washington's financial campaign against Tehran accelerates.
